---
name: Student workflow request
about: Ask for help improving a public-safe graduate thesis workflow
title: "[Student workflow]: "
labels: student-workflow
assignees: ""
---

## Student Stage

Choose one:

- [ ] No topic yet
- [ ] Vague direction or keyword
- [ ] Partial outline or chapter draft
- [ ] Near-final draft

## Public-Safe Context

Describe the situation using placeholders only. Do not include private thesis text, unpublished research data, interview transcripts, school-only forms, personal identifiers, or confidential workplace details.

Example:

> I am a Taiwan professional master's student with a vague keyword like "digital transformation." I need a safer way to turn it into seed-paper search terms and advisor questions.

## Current Blocker

What is blocking progress?

- [ ] I do not know how to narrow the topic.
- [ ] I do not know how to find seed papers.
- [ ] I do not know how to verify citations.
- [ ] I do not know how to turn advisor feedback into revisions.
- [ ] I need a final-defense risk check.

## Desired Output

What should the toolkit help produce?

- [ ] Topic brief
- [ ] Literature map workspace
- [ ] Evidence registry
- [ ] AI usage log
- [ ] Advisor question list
- [ ] Citation-candidate report
- [ ] Concept hierarchy or promise-delivery report
- [ ] DOCX render output

## Example Input

Use a fake or de-identified example only.

```text
Keyword:
Available public data:
Advisor preference:
Time limit:
What I need next:
```

## Acceptance Criteria

How would you know the workflow improvement works?

- [ ] It can be demonstrated with placeholder content.
- [ ] It does not require private files or paid services.
- [ ] It has a command, document, or prompt that a first-time student can follow.
- [ ] It keeps citations or evidence marked as unverified until checked.
